summary: Typosquat of @solana/web3.js with install-time host exfiltration
details: Package name impersonates the legitimate @solana/web3.js library under a fake @solana-labs scope. On install, package.json declares "postinstall": "node install.js", which executes automatically via npm install. install.js reads host identity (os.hostname(), process.platform) and issues outbound HTTP/HTTPS POST requests, with execSync and curl primitives also present in the script. The install hook fires without user consent and ships installer host fingerprints off-machine. The combination of name impersonation of a widely-used Solana library, a postinstall script, and host-info collection paired with outbound HTTP POST is a credential/host reconnaissance dropper pattern targeting developer machines.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
